channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Explosion at Qatar Gas Plant Leaves at Least 54 Injured
item.description: The blast happened at a key site for natural gas production. The authorities blamed a technical malfunction while operations were being restarted after the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A screen grab from a video showing an explosion at the Ras Laffan industrial zone in Qatar on Sunday.

item.title: First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks End, Mediators Say
item.description: The first round of negotiations between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland ended with a “roadmap” to reach a final deal within 60 days, Pakistani and Qatari mediators said.
item.dc:creator: Jiwoong Hong

item.title: Keir Starmer Delivers Speech From Downing Street
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain is making a speech as pressure has mounted for him to resign.

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain will meet with top cabinet members behind closed doors on Tuesday.

item.title: Right-Wing Outsider Appears to Win Colombia’s Election
item.description: President Trump had endorsed Abelardo De La Espriella, a lawyer with no previous political experience, in Colombia’s presidential election. He appeared headed for a slim victory.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Abelardo De La Espriella gave a speech from behind bulletproof glass on Sunday night in Barranquilla, a city in northern Colombia.

item.title: U.K. Live Updates: Starmer Announces Resignation as Prime Minister
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer stepped down as leader of the governing Labour Party. His decision clears the way for Britain’s seventh prime minister in the last decade.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain outside 10 Downing Street in London on Monday.

item.title: Staten Island Was Promised a Sea Wall After Sandy. It’s Still Waiting.
item.description: The U.S. Army fired the contractor building the 4.3-mile-long structure. More than 13 years ago, a hurricane killed 24 people in the borough.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Michael Appleton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Residents recovered items from their homes in the Oakwood Heights neighborhood of Staten Island days after Hurricane Sandy in 2012.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 22, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: He Provided Security to 3 Governors. Then He Snapped.
item.description: Lt. Ricardo Santos of the New Jersey State Police had faced questions about his judgment and conduct before he became the primary suspect in a double murder-suicide.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully

media:content.media:credit: Kristin Callahan/Ace Pictures/ZUMA Press, via Alamy
media:content.media:description: Lt. Ricardo Santos of the New Jersey State Police worked in an elite unit protecting dignitaries.

item.title: For Schlossberg, Quirky Charm and a Claim to Camelot May Not Be Enough
item.description: Jack Schlossberg, the grandson of John F. Kennedy, has found it difficult to overcome some of his perceived shortcomings in a high-profile House primary race.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg and Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, at a recent campaign event in Manhattan, has fallen to third place in recent polls of the Democratic primary in the 12th Congressional District.

item.title: Two Boys Arrested After Deadly School Shooting in the Philippines
item.description: Three people were killed and seven wounded after two ninth graders opened fire on Monday morning in Tacloban City, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See and Jason Gutierrez

item.title: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
item.description: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Dana Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alberto Carvalho was hired in 2021 to lead the Los Angeles Unified School District, the nation’s second-largest school district.

item.title: Word of the Day: equipoise
item.description: This word has appeared in seven articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Qué cambió tras casi cuatro meses de guerra? Según los analistas, casi nada
item.description: Ni la guerra ni el acuerdo eliminaron las principales amenazas que emanan de Irán, según numerosos analistas.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Rescatistas de la defensa civil y personal de seguridad en un edificio residencial de la zona de Dahiyeh, en las afueras del sur de Beirut, tras los ataques israelíes

item.title: Ten Years After Brexit, the Dismal Verdict Is In
item.description: Brexit was supposed to let Britain return to a time when it still counted as a global power. A decade later, the costs are blindingly apparent.
item.dc:creator: Philip Stephens

item.title: ‘The Bear’ and 7 More Shows to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The hit FX series reaches its fifth and final season. The BET Awards are live in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Geordon Wollner

media:content.media:description: Jeremy Allen White, left, and Ayo Edebiri in Season 4 of “The Bear.”

item.title: Oil Prices Fall as U.S.-Iran Talks Show Signs of Progress
item.description: Oil prices retreated after Iran’s foreign minister said there had been “major progress” toward ending the fighting in Lebanon during the first session of high-level talks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.description: tktktkt
item.dc:creator: Tina Hsu

media:content.media:credit: Tina Hsu for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A class about diffusion equipment operation taught by Kang Soo Jin.

item.title: Does This Radio Host Really Know Everyone in Wales?
item.description: Elis James thinks he has a friend in common with just about everyone else in Wales. Don’t believe him? Give him 60 seconds to prove it.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Radio host and comedian Elis James has taken the Welsh penchant for chatting and being social to new heights.

item.title: No Corrections: June 22, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, June 22, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Highway 1 Is One Way to See Coast. Swimming the Length of It Is Another.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, June 22, 2026.

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3 Premiere Recap: A Song of Water and Fire
item.description: The Dance of the Dragons got into full swing with no shortage of blood, flames and intrigue, much of it at sea.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: The Sea Snake (Steve Toussaint) wasn’t expecting an attack from the Triarchy, or to come under friendly fire.

item.description: A funnel cloud forming in Keenes, Ill., on Sunday. Several tornadoes were reported in the region.

item.title: Conductor de Tesla en piloto automático choca contra una casa y mata a una mujer
item.description: El conductor dijo a los investigadores que usaba el sistema de conducción automatizada del vehículo cuando este se salió de la vía y se estrelló en el condado de Harris, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:description: El tablero de un vehículo Tesla mientras utiliza un sistema de asistencia a la conducción, en California.

item.title: Tornadoes Reported in Southern Illinois Leave Trail of Damage
item.description: By Sunday night, there had already been at least 13 reports of tornadic activity or damage, a meteorologist said.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner

item.title: La presidenta del Banco Central de Rusia aparece tras 2 semanas de ausencia
item.description: Elvira Nabiullina dio un mensaje de tranquilidad en una rueda de prensa después de un paréntesis que evidenció profundas tensiones en la élite política y económica del país.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Oficina de prensa del Banco Central de Rusia, vía Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Elvira S. Nabiullina, la presidenta del Banco Central de Rusia, habló el viernes. La oficina de prensa del banco publicó esta foto del evento.

item.title: Boyle Heights Warehouse Fire Spreads Smoke Across Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters say they are making progress against the blaze, which began Wednesday at a cold-storage facility. But smoke continued to blow across the region, and the authorities reiterated air-quality warnings.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto

media:content.media:description: A warehouse fire at a cold-storage facility in Boyle Heights continued to burn and spread smoke across Los Angeles this weekend.

item.title: Resultados de las elecciones de Colombia: De la Espriella se perfila ganador
item.description: La victoria del candidato respaldado por Trump supondría un revés para la izquierda y otra victoria para la derecha en Latinoamérica.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters of Mr. De La Espriella in Barranquilla on Sunday.

item.title: Abelardo De La Espriella, Trump-Backed Rightist, Headed for Win in Colombia
item.description: A victory for Abelardo De La Espriella, a lawyer with no previous political experience, would be another win for the right in Latin America.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters of Mr. De La Espriella in Barranquilla on Sunday.

item.title: Margaret Kerry, Body and Soul of Disney’s Tinker Bell, Dies at 97
item.description: A film crew and illustrator meticulously documented her pirouettes and pouts, giving substance to a character depicted previously as a spotlight onstage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub

media:content.media:description: Margaret Kerry in 2018. Trained as a dancer, she became the model for the animated version of Tinker Bell in the 1953 Disney movie “Peter Pan.”

item.title: Oil Rises Amid Uncertainty Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Modest reactions in the oil and financial markets as Iran and the U.S. met in Switzerland for a first round of talks aimed at making a temporary cease-fire permanent.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Deadly Rise of Giant Trucks and S.U.V.s
item.description: The vehicles on American roads have grown larger — and they are killing thousands more pedestrians, a Times investigation found.
item.dc:creator: Michael H. Keller, Eli Murray, Danielle Ivory and Irineo Cabreros
item.category: Sports Utility Vehicles and Light Trucks

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 22, 2026
item.description: Victoria Fernandez Grande clears the bar.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Don’t be a puzzle hog. How about solving today’s crossword with a friend?

item.title: The Man Trumpifying FIFA
item.description: Gianni Infantino, the FIFA president, has unabashedly courted the U.S. president’s favor.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Gianni Infantino at the White House in 2025.

item.title: Mark Singer, Longtime Writer for The New Yorker, Dies at 75
item.description: He joined the magazine’s staff at 23. Among the subjects of his profiles were the magician Ricky Jay and a pre-politics Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

media:content.media:credit: Neilson Barnard/Getty Images for The New Yorker
media:content.media:description: Mark Singer in 2009 speaking at The New Yorker Festival. He began working at the magazine when he was 23 and was best known for his profiles.

item.title: Tesla Driver Using Autopilot Crashes Into Home in Texas and Kills a Woman, Officials Say
item.description: The driver told investigators he was using the vehicle’s automated-driver system when the vehicle left the roadway and crashed into a house in Harris County, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

media:content.media:description: The dashboard of a Tesla model using a driver-assistance system in California.

item.title: Trump dice que probablemente habrá que vaciar el estanque reflectante
item.description: El estanque se ha llenado de algas tras una renovación apresurada. Un atleta olímpico fue acusado de destruir propiedad del gobierno después de tocar uno de los restos de pintura azul que se desprendían del fondo.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:description: Unos trabajadores intentaron limpiar la proliferación de algas del estanque reflectante recién renovado en Washington este jueves.

item.title: Al líder supremo de Irán le disgusta el acuerdo con EE. UU., dice
item.description: El ayatolá Mojtaba Jameneí dijo que había dado su visto bueno al acuerdo, pero que no estaba de acuerdo con firmarlo, “por una cuestión de principios”.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Se muestran fotos de Mojtaba Jameneí, el líder supremo de Irán, durante un acto conmemorativo por las víctimas de la guerra, celebrado en Teherán en mayo.

item.title: California Governor Declares State of Emergency for L.A. Warehouse Fire
item.description: A fire that broke out on Wednesday at a cold storage facility in Los Angeles continued to burn on Sunday. Gov. Gavin Newsom declared an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Crews work to extinguish a warehouse fire in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: El secreto de Seth Rogen: ternura, marihuana y no competir con nadie
item.description: Con 30 años en Hollywood, el actor y director reflexiona sobre lo que hace funcionar un matrimonio, una amistad y una carrera que no deja de crecer.
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro

media:content.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: Hantavirus Quarantine Ends for 18 Americans Exposed on a Cruise Ship
item.description: No cases associated with the outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship have been confirmed in the U.S., health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Nebraska Medicine’s Davis Global Center in Omaha, where American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were under quarantine.

item.title: Uncertainty Remains Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iranian militaries have offered conflicting accounts over whether the crucial waterway is open.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to be a point of negotiation between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Fuels Hollywood’s Hottest Summer Since 2019
item.description: The Disney-Pixar sequel was expected to make $160 million in North America over the weekend, lifting the summer box office to $1.85 billion to date.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: San Antonio Mayor Says Kanye West’s July 4 Concert There Should Be Canceled
item.description: The mayor’s appeal comes after several of the rapper’s shows were canceled in Europe. A planned concert in Tampa, Fla., has also raised concern.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:credit: Ramon Van Flymen/Agence France-Presse, via Anp/Afp Via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, performing in the Netherlands this month.

item.title: As Vance Offers Iran ‘Outstretched Hand’ in Talks, Trump Threatens Bombing
item.description: The administration’s split message came as the United States and Iran began a new round of nuclear negotiations in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: From left, Steve Witkoff, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance and the Pakistani Army official Asim Munir at Lake Lucerne in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: Begoña Gómez, primera dama de España, irá a juicio por corrupción
item.description: Begoña Gómez está acusada de corrupción y tráfico de influencias por apoyar la postulación de empresas con las que tiene vínculos personales para un contrato público.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and José Bautista

media:content.media:description: Begoña Gómez y su esposo, Pedro Sánchez, el presidente del gobierno de España, en un acto celebrado este mes en Barcelona.

item.title: Man Dies at MSG in Fall From Balcony During Goose Concert
item.description: The band Goose was playing on Saturday night when the fall took place. The cause of the episode was not clear.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The exterior of Madison Square Garden in New York City, where a concertgoer died Saturday after falling from the balcony.

item.title: Tom Dreesen, Comic Who Kept Sinatra’s Crowds Laughing, Dies at 86
item.description: He rose from a hardscrabble childhood to form a pioneering interracial comedy duo. He later spent years opening for Frank Sinatra.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Comedy Store (Los Angeles, Calif, Comedy Club)

media:content.media:description: Tom Dreesen performing in 2004. He was Frank Sinatra’s opening act for 13 years and said, “From my vantage point, every night, there was no TV series, no radio show, no movie deal that compared to what I was experiencing.”

item.title: Severe Weather Expected in the Northeast on Monday
item.description: A storm system will bring a chance of heavy rain and damaging winds to Philadelphia, New York City and Washington. Forecasters warned of power outages and scattered flooding.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: How Basel Went From Art to Art Basel
item.description: Basel’s art history predates the world’s most prominent art fair by a few centuries.
item.dc:creator: Nina Siegal

item.title: World Cup Updates: Visiting Soccer Fans in Miami Encounter Beaches With Smelly Seaweed
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Saul Martinez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: South Beach in Miami on Friday. Large amounts of sargassum began washing up on South Florida beaches in April and May
